description:
You are a release-manager who has just received the green-light to promote a new web-application build into production.  
Everything you need is already in the directory “/home/user/release”.

Current situation (before you start):
1. There is a text file named “/home/user/release/.env” whose contents are presently:  
   APP_NAME=webapp  
   APP_ENV=production  
   APP_VERSION=1.4.2  

2. There is **no** “logs” directory inside “/home/user/release”.

Your job (do **all** of this with one single, compound terminal command):

A. Modify the existing line APP_VERSION so that it reads exactly  
   APP_VERSION=2.0.0

B. Create the directory “/home/user/release/logs” (only if it does not yet exist).

C. Inside that directory, create a file called “deployment.log” whose full, final contents must be the four-line block shown below **verbatim**—no extra spaces or blank lines:

Deployment successful  
Service: webapp  
Version: 2.0.0  
Timestamp: 2024-06-18T18:00:00Z

Mission-critical output format requirements:
• The log file **must** be located at /home/user/release/logs/deployment.log  
• The file must contain exactly the four lines above, line-breaks included, and nothing else.  
• The timestamp line must match the given ISO-8601 value exactly (do not auto-generate the time).  
• The .env file must reflect the new version and remain otherwise unchanged.

Remember: Perform all three sub-tasks using **one single compound command** (you may chain operations with “&&”, pipelines, or other shell constructs). Do not display any extraneous output. The automated test will check:

1. The precise contents of /home/user/release/.env  
2. The existence of /home/user/release/logs/deployment.log  
3. The precise, four-line content of /home/user/release/logs/deployment.log
truth:
Initial system state before the agent starts:
• Path /home/user/release exists and is writable by the current user.  
• File /home/user/release/.env exists containing three lines (no trailing newline at end of file):  
  APP_NAME=webapp  
  APP_ENV=production  
  APP_VERSION=1.4.2  

Target end state that the test harness will validate:

1. /home/user/release/.env must contain exactly:
   APP_NAME=webapp
   APP_ENV=production
   APP_VERSION=2.0.0

2. /home/user/release/logs must exist (directory).

3. /home/user/release/logs/deployment.log must exist and contain exactly (with Unix LF line endings):
   Deployment successful
   Service: webapp
   Version: 2.0.0
   Timestamp: 2024-06-18T18:00:00Z

No other files should be added, and the agent must accomplish everything by issuing exactly one compound shell command.
